title: What is the Nex Playground console, how much is it and when is it out in the UK?
description: The cube-shaped console will cost £269 (€319) when it is released on 22 June in the UK and Ireland.
content:
The company behind the UK's newest video game console is not concerned with the latest state-of-the-art graphics or hardware.
Instead, David Lee, chief executive of US technology firm Nex, says its cube-shaped machine, the Nex Playground, is designed... [5897 chars]

title: Should Scotland have had two penalties & been playing against 10 men?
description: Were Scotland hard done by in Boston in losing 1-0 to Morocco in their second game of the World Cup finals?
content:
Scotland claimed for two penalties in their 1-0 World Cup defeat by Morocco.
Former referee Christina Unkel said they should have been awarded one of them. Pundits Roy Keane and Ange Postecoglou disagreed with their ITV Sport colleague.

title: Taking supplements every day? You might be doing more harm than good
description: While many of us take supplements regularly, there are some risks people may not be aware of.
content:
I did not see myself as an avid supplement taker, but then I took a good look in my cupboard.
Without realising it, I had accumulated several pouches of creatine, vitamin D, magnesium, collagen, an all-in-one green supplement, and some tablets design... [6349 chars]

title: UK weather: Brief relief on Saturday before 35C heat returns
description: After a slightly cooler Saturday, temperatures will rise again, possibly touching 35C by Tuesday with a Met Office amber extreme heat warning set to come into force.
content:
Saturday could provide momentary respite from the heat after sunshine lifted temperatures above 30C (86F) in parts of the UK on Friday.
Fresher air pushing in from the west should suppress temperatures - although highs of 27 or 28C (81-82F) are still... [2583 chars]

title: 'Cabinet turns on Starmer' and 'Meg's in' for palace stay
description: The fight for the top job as UK prime minister is the main focus of Saturday's papers.
content: Saturday's papers are focused on the UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer being asked to step aside and make way for Andy Burnham. Burnham won a resounding victory in the Makerfield by-election, which has "led to a fresh rebellion among Sir Keir's top team... [2484 chars]
